item.title: Medusa Hackathon 2022：参与提示和指南 | Medusa Hackathon 2022: Participation Tips and Guidelines
item.description:
周六，我们启动了美杜莎黑客马拉松，开发者可以通过使用美杜莎创造一些很棒的东西来参与其中，并赢取高达 1,500 美元和超酷的赃物！

这篇文章为您提供了一些关于从哪里开始、一些项目示例以及参与时需要遵循的指南的提示。

项目示例

尽管美杜莎的可能性是无穷无尽的，但我们知道有时很难找到想法。 本节包含一些关于使用 Medusa 为 Hackathon 创建的项目的一般想法和示例。

直播店

建立一个实时电子商务商店是美杜莎的全部内容！ 参与黑客马拉松的一种方式是部署一个实时的美杜莎商店，并添加您自己的风格，包括使用现有的插件、店面或自己制作！

以前的博客文章中的一些示例，介绍了使用 Medusa 构建商店、集成插件和部署它：

电子商务店面

这部分意味着该解决方案是无头的，它允许您使用您喜欢的前端框架实现您想要的任何类型的店面。

如果您对其他前端框架有经验，想要自定义我们现有的店面，或者对我们现有的店面有更好的设计，您可以将其用作您的 Hackathon 项目。

之前关于使用 Medusa 构建店面的博客文章中的一些示例：

电子商务应用

与之前的项目类型类似，您可以将任何类型的移动应用程序与 Medusa 服务器连接以创建电子商务移动应用程序。

上一篇博文中的一个例子：

  • 插件

    插件可用于添加新功能或将美杜莎与第三方服务集成。

    以前博客文章中的一些示例和我们文档中现有的插件：

    管理面板

    您可以构建一个具有更多功能和酷炫设计的新管理面板，也可以添加到现有的管理面板中。

    核心定制

    由于 Medusa 是一个开源平台，您可以将其拆开并更改为您需要的任何内容。 您可以自定义其架构、实体或业务模型。

    核心自定义的一些示例可以是：

    • 创建订阅业务模型。
    • 创建市场业务模型。

    有创意

    创意是每场黑客马拉松的核心。 对于美杜莎黑客松，我们很高兴看到您能想出的所有创意项目。

    我还需要更多的想法

    在接下来的几周内，我们将在我们的博客上分享 Medusa 提交的不同示例，包括自定义店面、添加集成、创建电子商务应用程序等等。

    请留意这些文章。 您可以在Twitter和LinkedIn上关注我们，或加入我们的Discord 社区，随时了解新文章。

    我从哪说起呢？

    如果您有一些想法但不确定如何使用 Medusa 来实现它们，或者您仍在学习 Medusa，本部分将分享一些有关您可以从哪里开始的资源！

    所有项目类型

    • 美杜莎服务器快速入门指南。
    • 直播商店

      电子商务店面和移动应用程序

      插件

      管理面板

      • 核心定制

        我需要遵循哪些准则？

        您的 README 页面必须正确展示您的项目是什么以及如何安装和使用它。 您可以查看此指南存储库，了解有关您的 README 必须包含的内容的更多详细信息。

        如何提交我的项目？

        要提交您的项目，请在此讨论主题中回复您的存储库链接。 您的存储库必须遵循准则才能被接受。

        我们有一个Hackathon 存储库，它将所有存储库提交组合在一起。 我们的团队将定期更新此存储库，以包含经证明符合我们的指导方针的提交。

        我有更多问题或在开发过程中需要帮助

        如果您在创建项目时还有其他问题或需要帮助，可以加入我们的Discord 社区。 在这个 Discord 服务器上，您可以：

        • 在qa-hackathon论坛上提问与 Hackathon 相关的问题。
        • 加入团队或在teams-n-projects论坛上查找项目创意。
        • 在help论坛上获得开发过程中的任何帮助。


原文:

On Saturday, we kicked off the Medusa Hackathon where developers can participate by creating something awesome using Medusa and win up to $1,500 and cool swag!

This post gives you some tips on where to start, some project examples, and what guidelines you need to follow when you participate.

Examples of Projects

Although the possibilities are endless with Medusa, we know it can be hard to find ideas sometimes. This section includes some general ideas and examples for projects to create with Medusa for the Hackathon.

Live stores

Building a live ecommerce store is all what Medusa is about! One way to participate in the Hackathon is to deploy a live Medusa store, and add in your own touches, including using existing plugins, storefronts, or making your own!

Some examples from previous blog posts on building a store with Medusa, integrating plugins, and deploying it:

Ecommerce Storefronts

Medusa provides bespoke ecommerce infrastructure. Part of this means the solution is headless, which allows you to implement any type of storefront you want with your preferred frontend framework.

Medusa currently has two starter storefronts: one with Next.js and one with Gatsby.

If you are experienced with other frontend frameworks, want to customize our existing storefronts, or have a better design in mind for our existing storefronts, you can use that as your Hackathon project.

Some examples from previous blog posts on building storefronts with Medusa:

Ecommerce Apps

Similar to the previous project type, you can connect any type of mobile app with the Medusa server to create an Ecommerce Mobile app.

An example from a previous blog post:

  • React Native app with Medusa.

Plugins

Medusa allows developers to integrate additional features using plugins. Plugins can be used to add new features or integrate Medusa with third-party services.

Some examples from previous blog posts and existing plugins in our documentation:

Admin Panel

Medusa provides an admin panel that allows merchants to manage their store’s settings, products, orders, and more.

You can either build a new admin panel with more features and a cool design, or you can add to the existing one.

Core Customization

Since Medusa is an open-source platform, you can take it apart and change it into anything that you need. You can customize its architecture, entities, or business model.

Some examples of core customizations can be:

  • Create a subscription business model.
  • Create a marketplace business model.

Be creative

Creativity is at the core of every Hackathon. For the Medusa Hackathon, we’re excited to see all the creative projects that you can come up with.

Some example of a creative project with Medusa is Medusa.Express, which allows merchants to easy share product links with one-page checkout functionality.

I still need more ideas

Over the next few weeks, we’ll be sharing on our blog different examples of Medusa submissions including customizing storefronts, adding integrations, creating ecommerce apps, and more.

Be on the lookout for these articles. You can follow us on Twitter and LinkedIn, or join our Discord community to always be informed of new articles.

Where do I start?

If you have some ideas but are unsure how to implement them with Medusa, or you are still learning about Medusa, this section shares some resources on where you can get started!

All Project Types

  • Medusa server Quickstart guide.
  • Beginner Guide to Medusa.

Live Stores

Ecommerce Storefronts and Mobile Apps

Plugins

Admin Panel

  • Medusa’s Admin API Reference.
  • Medusa JS Client Overview.

Core Customization

What are some guidelines I need to follow?

Your README page must properly showcase what your project is and how to install and use it. You can check out this guideline repository for more details on what your README must include.

How do I submit my project?

To submit your project, reply with the link to your repository in this discussion thread. Your repository must follow the guidelines to be accepted.

We have a Hackathon repository that groups together all repository submissions. This repository will be regularly updated by our team to include submissions that are proven to follow our guidelines.

I have more questions or I need help during the development

If you have any more questions or you need help while creating your project, you can join our Discord community. On this Discord server you can:

  • Ask questions related to the Hackathon on the qa-hackathon forum.
  • Join teams or find project ideas on the teams-n-projects forum.
  • Get any help during your development on the help forum.
item.author: shahednasser

item.title: 描述我的沙盒 | Describing My Sandbox
item.description:
今年我一直忙于通过重建一组开发技能来执行我的 #100DaysOfCode 挑战，我可以使用这些技能来使用微服务实现产品。 这项工作产生了一个相当强大的混合用途沙箱。 作为构建综合公共文档门户的努力的一部分，周末我开始着手编写一份文档来描述我的沙盒架构。 下面是我制作的第一个架构图，从顶层描述了构成我的沙箱的服务以及它们是如何分区或组织的：

使用Cloudflare 惊人的DNS 服务，我的沙箱分布在两个域中：

  • 我使用此区域主要在我的家中提供内容，以及在云中提供实验性或成本过高的服务。
  • 知识空间
    • 传输：传输是一个 BitTorrent 客户端。 我使用的传输的具体实现包括 OpenVPN ，允许我安全地路由该流量。
    • 在以营利为目的的情况下，我会在云中运行此服务，但我正在利用现有资源来运行这样一个次要服务。

    我目前有超过 100 万条个人数据记录，这些记录来自十几个来源，包括第三方服务（ Twitter 、 Facebook 、 Discogs ）、手动数据输入（新闻稿和新闻文章、明信片）和Apple 的 Healthkit （锻炼、消耗的卡路里） ， 心率）。

  • 联系方式：虽然构建很酷的东西很棒，但有时您只需要一个简单的静态内容网站工具。 联系就是这些例子之一。 布鲁斯传奇：社会实验的营销网站。 当我参加音乐节时，我通常会带上一只大型充气恐龙（布鲁斯）来人群冲浪头条表演，而在深夜结束时，他总是会和他在人群中随便找到的人一起回家。 状态页面：状态页面是 SaaS 产品的一个常见功能， BetterUptime让对我的资源实施监控变得非常容易。
  • 公共文档门户： Gitbook使我能够在我的 Github 项目中创作公共文档，并以一种像样的方式轻松地提供它。 我使用这个门户来共享我发布的开源项目的文档，以及架构文档和操作指南。

到目前为止，您对我的沙盒有疑问或意见吗？ 把它们放在下面⬇️


感谢您访问 DEV，我们非常努力地培养这个伟大的社区，并希望您能加入我们。 如果您想创建一个帐户，您可以在这里注册。

一旦暂停，theaccordance 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，该协议将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，所有的帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果该协议没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记该协议：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记该协议将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

I've been busy this year executing my take on the #100DaysOfCode challenge by rebuilding a set of development skills that I can use to implement products using micro-services. That work has resulted in a rather robust mixed-use sandbox. As part of my effort to build out a comprehensive public documentation portal, over the weekend I set about starting a document to describe my Sandbox's Architecture. Below is the first architecture diagram I've produced, describing from a top level the services which make up my sandbox & how they're zoned or organized:

Using Cloudflare's amazing DNS service, my Sandbox is spread across two domains:

  • Knowhere.space: Knowhere is my edge-computing zone, with the DNS routing to a NAS server or Raspberry PIs within my home. I use this zone to serve content primarily within my home, as well as services which are either experimental, or cost-prohibitive in the cloud.
  • Mainwaring.dev: Mainwaring.dev encompasses all of my cloud computing services from a variety of different providers, including DigitalOcean, Vercel, Carrd.co and others.

Knowhere.space

  • Traefik: Traefik acts as an ingress for my homelab, enabling me to route traffic to the other services I have running on the edge without overly exposing my Firewall with open ports.
  • PiHole: PiHole is a DNS Sinkhole I added to reduce the number of ads and trackers served to devices on my local network.
  • Plex: Plex is my personal Netflix, serving content I have stored on my local network.
  • YAC Reader: Comics are a guilty pleasure of mine, and YAC Reader enables me to serve digital comics to my iPad with ease.
  • Transmission: Transmission is a BitTorrent client. The specific implementation of Transmission I use includes OpenVPN, allowing me to securely route that traffic.
  • Lighthouse: Spotify's open-source Lighthouse Audit Service integrates my Backstage.io implementation with Google Lighthouse. In a for-profit situation, I would run this service in the cloud, but I'm taking advantage of existing resources I have to run such a minor service.

Mainwaring.dev

  • API: A very robust REST server built using the @strapijs. I currently have over 1 million records of personal data stored from more than dozen sources, including third-party services (Twitter, Facebook, Discogs), manual data entry (press releases & news articles, postcards) and Apple's Healthkit (Workouts, Calories Burned, Heart Rate).
  • Backstage (server): Backstage is an open-source project from Spotify which enables teams to build developer portals and customize them in a way to aggregate data from all the various tools a team uses.
  • Chartbrew (server): Chartbrew is open-source reporting service which really makes it easy to visualize data.
  • Backstage (website): The Backstage Frontend is a separate process from the backend, so I opted to host it on Vercel.
  • Chartbrew (website): Similar to Backstage, the Chartbrew frontend is a separate process which I host on Vercel.
  • Contact: While it's awesome to build cool things, sometimes you just need a simple website tool for static content. Contact is one of these examples. This branded-alternative to LinkTree is used as a digital business card, providing my audience with a list of options to learn about myself and make contact.
  • The Legend of Bruce: A marketing website for a social experiment. When I attend music festivals, I typically bring a large inflatable dinosaur (Bruce) to crowd-surf a headlining act, and at the end of the night he always goes home with a random person he found in the crowd. Bruce now includes a QR code linking back to this page, which serves as a Call to Action to share pictures or videos people take with Bruce on Instagram.
  • Status Page: Status Pages are a common feature for SaaS products, and BetterUptime made it really easy to implement monitoring over my resources.
  • Public Documentation Portal: Gitbook enables me to author public documentation within my Github Project and easily serve it in a presentable way. I use this portal to share documentation for open-source projects I publish, as well as Architecture documents and how-to guides.

Do you have questions or comments about my sandbox so far? Drop them below ⬇️

 
Thanks for visiting DEV, we’ve worked really hard to cultivate this great community and would love to have you join us. If you’d like to create an account, you can sign up here.

item.title: 软件开发人员实际上在做什么？ | What Do Software Developers Actually Do?
item.description:
要开始设计软件解决方案，我相信我们需要一个简单的单行定义来定义软件开发人员的工作。

这个定义将作为我们在本书中不断返回以确定我们所处位置的基础。

软件开发的首要原则

软件开发人员编写代码以将手动流程转变为自动化流程。

这就是我们所做的一切。

这看起来太简单了吗？

您可能认为这太简单了，无法涵盖所有​​软件。
但是，如果您从抽象的角度考虑任何软件，甚至是游戏，您会发现它实际上只是一个手动过程，已经变成了自动化过程。

吃豆人作为手动过程

看看吃豆人视频游戏。 最初，Pac-Man 可能是一款棋盘游戏，玩家可以掷骰子来移动 Pac-Man 和幽灵。 玩家可以决定吃豆人的方向，你可以旋转一个旋转器来获取每个幽灵移动的方向。 当你沿着棋盘上的路径移动吃豆人时，你只需计算他每掷骰子吃了多少个点。

接下来，您将为每个幽灵掷骰子并旋转微调器以指示移动方向。 每转一圈，鬼魂都可能降落在吃豆人令牌所在的地方并摧毁他。

当然，目标是让你的吃豆人在被鬼吃掉之前吃掉所有的点。 既然我们知道了吃豆人应该如何工作的手册细节，那么编写视频游戏就会容易得多。

视频游戏只是使这个过程自动化。 代码是“掷骰子”和“旋转微调器”来移动幕后的鬼魂。 它还会每次更新幽灵的位置，使其看起来好像在移动。 你看？ 这一切真的只是自动化。

但是，您已经了解了与软件开发相关的另一个重要公理。 明天我们将看看第二个重要的公理。

你怎么看？

您是否同意软件开发只是办公自动化？

原文:

To get started with designing Software Solutions, I believe we need a simple, single line definition of what software developers do.

This definition will serve as a foundation we will continually return to in this book to determine where we are at.

First Tenet of Software Development

Software developers write code to turn manual processes into automated processes.

That’s really all we do.

Does That Seem Too Simple?

You may think that is too simple to cover all software.
However, if you consider any software, even a game, from an abstract viewpoint, you’ll find that it is really just a manual process that has been turned into an automated process.

Pac-Man As A Manual Process

Take a look at the Pac-Man video game. Originally, Pac-Man could’ve been a board game where players roll dice to move Pac-Man and the ghosts. The player could decide the direction for Pac-Man and you could spin a spinner to get the direction for each ghost movement. As you move the Pac-Man along the path on the board you would simply tally up how many dots he eats with each roll of the die.

Next, you’d roll the dice for each ghost and spin the spinner to indicate movement direction. Each turn the ghosts could possibly land where the Pac-Man token is and destroy him.

Of course, the object would be to get your Pac-Man to eat all of the dots before he is eaten by the ghosts. (See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pac-Man^ for more on the Pac-Man video game.)

Now that we know the manual details of how Pac-Man should work, it would be far easier to write the video game.

The video game simply automates this process. The code is “rolling the dice” and “spinning the spinner” to move the ghosts behind the scenes. It’s also updating the location of the ghosts each time making it seem as if they are moving. You see? It all really is just automation.

However, you’ve learned another important axiom related to software development. We'll take a look at that second important axiom tomorrow.

What Do You Think?

Do you agree that Software Development is just Office Automation?

Once suspended, raddevus will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, raddevus will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by raddevus will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If raddevus is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag raddevus:

raddevus consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging raddevus will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: raddevus

item.title: 极品飞车：从 Webpack 4 迁移到 Vite | Need for speed: Migrating from Webpack 4 to Vite
item.description:
我们将 Web 应用程序的代码库从Webpack 4迁移到Vite ，原因很简单：速度🏃🏃🏃。

由于 Vite 相对较新，我们可以分享它是稳定的，并且对我们作为开发人员的生活产生了巨大的影响。 此外，这一举措很简单，而且没有花很长时间——尽管我们在Swimm的团队在此过程中吸取了一些惨痛的教训。

这里有更多关于我们为什么首先进行转换的原因。

什么是维特？

为什么我们决定从 Webpack 迁移

我们在 Swimm 使用 Webpack 4 两年多了，因为它在 Vue CLI 中使用，这是我们用于应用程序客户端的服务。 但大约六个月前，当我们的代码库扩展并变得更加复杂时，Webpack 拖慢了我们的速度，我们决定我们需要做出改变。

尽管 Webpack 5 已经推出了几年，但将 Vue CLI 从 v4 迁移到 v5 引入了一些重大变化，使得迁移变得非常具有挑战性。

随着我们的 Web 应用程序变得越来越大，并且我们在代码库中有很多文件，这使得 HMR 变得如此缓慢，有时甚至会崩溃。

  • 为本地开发启动 Webpack 最多需要两分钟。
  • 从 Webpack 迁移到 Vite：挑战

    整个迁移到 Vite 只用了几天时间。 大部分工作由四个开发人员在一天内完成，一些前期工作在前几天完成。

    虽然我们能够快速迁移，但我们必须立即克服一些技术难题。

    项目分离
    首先，我们的代码库需要一些重组。

    除了 Web 应用程序之外， Swimm 还有几个项目，包括IDE 插件和Swimm 的 GitHub 应用程序，并且 Webpack 的配置与其中大多数相关。 因为 Vite 是为 web 项目设计的，所以我们必须提取项目之间的共享代码，以便 web 应用程序是隔离和独​​立的。

    环境变量和配置文件
    其次，我们必须清理我们的环境变量和配置文件，包括令牌、URL 和 .env 文件。 特别是，我们的 TypeScript 和 eslint 配置必须重新调整。

    此外，Vite 中的环境处理方式与 Webpack 中的非常不同。 具体来说，Vite 有自己的一组默认值和一个新的命名空间。 我们认为，像我们这样的其他开发团队将需要进行广泛的迁移和配置更改工作，才能在 Vite 的环境期望范围内工作。

    使用 Swimm 迁移到 Vite

    从 Webpack 迁移到 Vite 的过程中有很多代码更改。 那里没有惊喜。

    由于我们所有的代码都记录在 Swimm 中，这意味着我们能够确保我们的文档保持最新、最新和相关。 而且，当然，我们有一个地方可以放置我们不想忘记迁移的所有项目。

    因此，除了与代码更改相关的 Swimm 文档外，我们现在还有一个“我们如何使用 Vite 文档”供我们在 Swimm 的团队阅读和使用，因为我们将继续轻松地进行迁移。

    迁移到 Vite：轻松获胜

    一旦迁移的挑战过去了，我们立即看到 Vite 获胜。

    速度
    在开发模式下，无论项目规模有多大，都不会影响 Vite 的速度。

    • 原生集成和效率
      它比使用 Webpack 快几个数量级。 此外，Vite 只支持现代浏览器，因此没有额外的代码来支持许多开发团队没有使用的旧版浏览器。

      但是您不必将 Vue 与 Vite 一起使用。 其他技术细节

      如果您正在考虑从 Webpack 迁移到 Vite，请记住其他几点：

      • 垫片和填充物。 与 Webpack 4 和更早的版本不同，您不会使用 Vite 为 Node.JS 模块获得任何自动填充程序或 polyfills。 因此，对旧的混合 Node.JS/Browser 模块的支持可能有点困难。 你不能轻易地导入任何东西并期望它能够工作。 而且，如果您有一些对任务至关重要的遗留 Node.JS 模块，它将带来您需要考虑的挑战。

      • 捆绑。 在捆绑方面，Vite 是一个通用的解决方案，它的处理方式略有不同。 它不仅仅是一个文件观察器、Web 服务器和捆绑器； 它进行按需编译。 当浏览器请求更新的 JS 文件时，Vite 会实时及时地交付它们。 由于 esbuild，它非常快。 不需要复杂的捆绑器解决方案，它只通过 HMR 替换更改的文件。

      • 项目配置。 项目配置也是需要注意的。 我们在模板方面遇到了各种各样的问题，不得不深入研究文档。 如果可以，我们建议您坚持配置默认值和模板，并且只有在您知道自己在做什么的情况下才更改配置。

      底线

      从 Webpack 迁移到 Vite 非常值得，我们对结果非常满意。 如果您的代码存储库具有跨项目的共享资源和依赖项，则从 Webpack 迁移到 Vite 可能会带来一些挑战。 但是，如果您的代码很好地隔离和独立，那么您希望可以轻松地使用它。

      如果 Webpack 拖慢了你的速度，我们认为迁移到 Vite 是非常值得的投资。



原文:

We migrated our web app’s codebase from Webpack 4 to Vite for one simple reason: speed 🏃🏃🏃.

Since Vite is relatively new, we can share that it’s stable and has made a tremendous difference in our lives as developers. Plus, the move was straightforward and didn’t take long—though our team here at Swimm learned some hard lessons along the way.

Here’s a bit more about why we made the switch in the first place.

What is Vite?

Vite is a Javascript build tool designed for speed. Vite consists of two major parts: a dev server that uses native ES modules that makes Hot Module Replacement incredibly faster, and a build command that uses Rollup behind the scenes.

Why we decided to migrate from Webpack

We used Webpack 4 at Swimm for over two years as it’s used in Vue CLI, which is the service we use for our app’s client-side. But about six months ago, we decided we needed to change when Webpack slowed us down as our codebase expanded and became more complex.

Though Webpack 5 has been available for a couple of years, migrating Vue CLI from v4 to v5 introduced several breaking changes that made the migration quite challenging.

Webpack’s performance was a deal breaker
Webpack’s Hot Module Replacement (HMR) works well until you have too many files in your codebase. As our web app became larger and larger and we had many files in the codebase, it made HMR so slow it would sometimes break.

  • Launching Webpack for local development took upwards of two minutes at best.
  • HMR in Webpack took about 10 seconds.

Moving from Webpack to Vite: the challenges

The entire move to Vite took just a couple of days. Most of the work was done in one day by four devs, with some pre-work done in the days prior.

While we were able to migrate quickly, we had to overcome some technical difficulties right off the bat.

Project separation
First off, our codebase needed some restructuring.

Swimm has several projects in addition to the web app including IDE plugins and Swimm’s GitHub app, and Webpack’s configuration was relevant for most of them. Because Vite is intended for web projects, we had to extract the shared code between the projects so that the web app would be isolated and independent.

Environment variables and configuration files
Second, we had to clean up our environment variables and config files including tokens, URLs, and .env files. In particular, our TypeScript and eslint configuration had to be readjusted.

Also, environments in Vite are handled very differently than in Webpack. Specifically, Vite has its own set of defaults and a new namespace. We imagine that other dev teams, like us, will need to go through extensive migration and configuration change work to work within Vite’s environmental expectations.

Using Swimm to Migrate to Vite

There were many code changes during the process of migrating from Webpack to Vite. No surprises there.

And since all of our code is documented in Swimm, this meant that we were able to ensure that our docs stayed up to date, current, and relevant. And, of course, we had a place to put all of the items we didn’t want to forget about the migration.

So, in addition to the Swimm documentation connected to the code changes, we also now have a “How We Use Vite doc” for our team at Swimm to read and use as we continue to ease into the migration.

Moving to Vite: the easy wins

Once the challenges for the migration were behind us, we saw the Vite wins immediately.

Speed
No matter how big the project size is in development mode, it doesn't impact Vite’s speed.

  • Vite launches in less than one second (~700 milliseconds).
  • HMR in Vite takes less than one second.

Native integrations and efficiency
Vite was written by the same developers as Vue, so we gained from a lot of the native integrations and synergies between the two. Vite also uses esbuild and doesn’t require bundling in pre-production environments, which makes local development so fast. It’s orders of magnitude faster than using Webpack. Also, Vite only supports modern browsers, so there’s no additional code to support legacy browsers many dev teams aren’t using.

But you don’t have to use Vue with Vite. Vite is completely separate so you can use it with React, Angular, and others however they best fit your project needs. Vite also has a lot of official plugins and many more community plugins.

Other technical details

A few other points to keep in mind if you’re considering migrating from Webpack to Vite:

  • Shims and Polyfills. Unlike Webpack 4 and earlier versions, you don’t get any automatic shims or polyfills for Node.JS modules with Vite. So, support for older mixed Node.JS/Browser modules can be a bit difficult. You can’t easily import anything and just expect it to work. And if you have some legacy Node.JS module that’s mission-critical, it will present challenges you’ll need to consider.

  • Bundling. When it comes to bundling, Vite is a general solution that does things a little differently. It’s not just a file watcher, web server, and bundler; it does on-demand compilation. As a browser requests updated JS files, Vite delivers them live and just in time. It’s super fast because of esbuild. There’s no need for complicated bundler solutions, and it only replaces the changed file via HMR.

  • Project configuration.Project configuration is also something to watch out for. We had all sorts of issues with templates and had to go deep into the documentation. We recommend you stick to configuration defaults and templates if you can and only change the configuration if you know what you’re doing.

Bottom Line

Migrating from Webpack to Vite was well worth it, and we are very happy with the results. The migration from Webpack to Vite may present some challenges if your code repository has shared resources and dependencies across projects. But if your code is well isolated and independent, you’ll hopefully have an easy time with it.

We think the migration to Vite is well worth the investment if Webpack has slowed you down.
item.author: swimm_io

item.title: 引擎盖下的 GIT | GIT Under the Hood
item.description:
作为一名软件工程师，我们很多人都使用 Git，只有少数人知道确切的内部结构或幕后发生的事情。

当您进行文件更改并提交时，内部会发生什么？

有时您可能会遇到不了解 git 在后端如何工作的情况下不知道如何解决的问题。

在这篇文章中，我将通过简单的例子分享对 Git 如何在底层工作的深入理解。

注意：本文旨在了解 git 如何使用低级命令工作以及幕后发生的事情。 它不是使用 Git 的指南

初始化 Git 存储库

让我们从使用git init命令初始化 Git 存储库开始。

它创建一个名为.git的隐藏文件夹，其中包含不同的文件和文件夹

此文件夹包含 git 到版本控制所需的所有信息和配置。 这里我们主要关注对象文件夹，其中将包含我们文件系统的跟踪信息。

工作状态 -这是您在文件中添加、删除或修改某些内容的时间。 在传统方式中，这些是“未跟踪/未分阶段的更改”

暂存状态 -这是您通知 Git 更改并告诉 Git 记录该更改的时间。 俗称暂存区。 暂存更改可以使用git add命令完成。 即使在暂存后也可以修改文件，这让您可以看到处于工作状态和暂存状态的文件。

提交阶段 -这是 Git以 git 对象的形式将您的工作状态更改保存在 .git 文件夹中的阶段。 传统方式是使用git commit命令实现的。

这里让我们看看 Git 内部/幕后 Git 如何存储更改

这意味着如果您在 Git 中插入任何类型的内容，它将返回给您一个唯一的密钥，以便您以后可以使用它来检索内容。

唯一密钥只不过是一种安全散列算法 (SHA-1)，它是一种加密散列函数，它接受输入并生成160 位（20 字节）散列值，通常呈现为 40 位长的十六进制* 。 使用这个哈希函数，我们可以引用任何文件内容、提交等。

内容的单个空格变化会导致不同的Hash值/唯一键生成

生成的每个 Hash 值都会与 Git 中的 git 对象相关联，以了解开发人员在工作阶段所做的更改，并将其保存在提交阶段。

现在是 Git 对象的时候了。 这些是 git 保存必要数据所需的唯一对象类型

  • 树- 目录信息存储为树。 树可以包含一组 Blob，甚至它也可以包含一组带有 Blob 的树。 树是 Git 中文件夹/目录的表示形式
  • 提交- 借助提交对象类型，您可以保存我们项目的不同版本
  • 带注释的标签- 它实际上是指向特定提交的持久文本指针

让我们创建一个名为gitsample.txt 的示例文件。 让我们暂存并提交它，看看文件如何作为对象类型存储在 .git 文件夹中

根据上面的解释，我们提交文件时会创建三种对象类型。

用于存储内容的 Blob，具有在文件创建/更改时创建的最新 blob 信息的树，下一个是提交对象类型，具有创建的最新树的信息

对于对象类型，可以像这样假设层次结构

重现上述场景的步骤

  • 在 git 存储库中创建一个名为 gitsample.txt 的文件。 在文件中保存“Hello World”

  • 在指向 git repo 文件夹的 git bash 中运行git status命令。 正如我们之前讨论的那样，更改/创建的文件将处于工作状态或也称为未跟踪更改，因此现在gitsample.txt将显示为未跟踪更改。

  • 下一步是通过运行命令git add 将文件从工作状态移动到暂存状态。 在此命令之后运行 git status。 还要观察一件事，即在 .git/objects 文件夹中，将使用唯一键/SHA 1 键作为文件名创建新的 blob 对象。 前 2 个哈希键数字将用于文件夹名称。

    结合文件夹名和文件名将是 gitsample.txt 文件的哈希键。 所以哈希键是3ee2d77fee2bc1ac806a945df84ad530a5de6fd8

    • 为确保这一点，请运行 git 低级命令git cat-file -p 。 这应该检索 gitsample.txt 中使用的内容“Hello World”

    • 接下来从暂存状态它应该移动到提交状态，

    要提交，我们必须运行命令git commit -m “Initial Commit”。

    • 当更改移动到提交状态时，它会创建其他 2 个对象树和提交对象。
      • 树还将生成自己的新哈希键，并将保存在暂存期间创建的 blob 对象的文件名和哈希键
      • 提交也将生成自己的哈希键，它将保存新创建的树对象哈希键和提交消息。

    为了确保这些是树和提交对象，使用-t扩展名和相应的哈希键运行低级命令git cat-file 。 它将显示对象的类型

    • 随着 .git/objects 文件夹中的这些对象更改，.git/refs 文件夹中的 HEAD 文件将有一个文件命名分支名称master ，其中包含最新的提交哈希键。

    当同一文件发生更改并提交时会发生什么？

    • 再次重复相同的过程，这将使用它们自己的哈希键创建新的 blob、提交和树。
    • 新分支创建并签出时会发生什么？

  • 包起来

    我希望这篇文章能让你清楚地了解 Git 如何在后台工作。 并且希望您在阅读本文后能够了解 git 中最关键的部分。

    如果您已经达到了这一点，感谢您的阅读。 我是 Abilash S，您可以查看我关于这些平台技术的最新帖子。 随意连接任何这些平台

    CodeHunt Channel Youtube CodeHunt 频道 Youtube

    Abi Aradhya - Medium Abi Aradhya - 中等

    JavaScript is not available. JavaScript 不可用。



原文:

# Git Under the hood

“Git is modern distributed version control system helps developers or anyone who want to keep track of changes in their file system” We can name it as a timeline of our project from beginning to end.

As an software engineers lot of us use Git ,only few know the exact internals or what happens under the hood.

what happens internally when you do a file change and commit it?

How is git tracking all the changes occurred for the file?

Sometime you may run into issues which you won’t know how to resolve without understanding how git works in the backend.

In this article, I am sharing a deep dive understanding on How Git works under the hood by taking simple examples.

Note : This article is to understand how git works using low level commands and what happens behind the scenes. It is not a guide to work with Git

Initialize a Git Repository

Lets start with initializing a Git repository using git init command.

It creates a hidden folder called .git which contains different files and folders inside

This folder contains all information and configuration needed for git to version control. Here we are mainly concentrated on objects folder where this will contain the tracking information of our file system.

Git 3 states or repository states

Working State - This is when you add, remove or modify something in the file. Git will notice the change but you will not formerly inform Git to save the changes. In traditional way these are “Untracked/ Not Staged Changes”

Staging State - This is when you inform Git of the change and tell Git to take note of that change. Commonly Known as Staging area. Staging changes can be done using git add command. Modification of file can be done even after staging it, which lets you see the file both in Working state and staged state.

Committed Stage - This is the stage where Git has saved your Working state changes in .git folder in the form of git objects. In Traditional way It is achieved using git commit command.

Here lets look into the Git Internals/ Behind the scenes How Git stores the changes

Git is a content-addressable file system or a simple key value data file system. That means If you insert any kind of content into the Git It will return you hand you back with a unique key so you can use that later to retrieve the content.

Unique Key is nothing but a Secure hash algorithm (SHA-1), which is a cryptographic hash function that takes an input and produces a 160-bit (20-byte) hash value typically rendered as a hexadecimal which is 40 digits long*.*

Using this Hash function we can reference any file content, commits etc.

A single space change in content will lead to different Hash value/ Unique key generation

Each Hash value generated will be linked with the git objects in Git to understand the changes made by developer in working stage to save it in committed stage.

Now its time for Git Objects. Git File system uses 4 types of git objects. These are the only object types required for git to save the necessary data

  • Blob - any type of files it may be photo, video or any file extensions is stored as a blob
  • Tree - Information of Directories is stores as a Tree. Tree can contain a set of Blobs and even it can contain set of trees with blobs. Tree is representation of folder/directory in Git
  • Commit - With the help of commit object type, You can save different versions of our project
  • Annotated Tag - Its actually persistent text pointer to specific commit

Lets create a sample file named gitsample.txt. lets stage and commit it and see how the file is stored in .git folder as an object types

According to above explanation there will be three object types created when we commit the file.

Blob to store the content, tree which has information of the latest blob which was created when file is created/Changed and next is Commit object type which has information of the latest tree created

Hierarchy can be assumed like this for object types

Steps to reproduce the above scenario

  • Create a file named gitsample.txt inside the git repository. Save “Hello World” inside the file

  • Run git status command in git bash pointing to the git repo folder. As we previously discussed the file changed/ created will be in the Working state or also known as Untracked changes so now here gitsample.txt will be shown as an untracked change.

  • Next step is to move file from Working state to Staging state by running command git add . Run git status after this command. gitsample.txt will be moved to Staging state.

One more thing to observe i.e. in .git/objects folder new blob object will be created with a unique key/ SHA 1 key as a file name. First 2 numbers of hash key will be used for a folder name.

Combining both folder name and file name will be the hash key of gitsample.txt file. so the hash key will be 3ee2d77fee2bc1ac806a945df84ad530a5de6fd8

  • To ensure this run a git low level command git cat-file -p . This should retrieve the content “Hello World” used in gitsample.txt

  • Next from Staging state it should be moved to Committed State,

To Commit we have to run the command git commit -m “Initial Commit”.

  • When changes are moved to committed state it creates other 2 objects Tree and commit object.
    • Tree will also have its own new Hash key generated and It will be holding the file name and hash key of the blob object created during staging
    • Commit will also have its own Hash key generated and it will be holding the the tree objects hash key which newly got created and the commit message.

To ensure these are the tree and commit objects run the low level command git cat-file using -t extension and respective hash keys. It will show the type of the object

  • With these object changes in .git/objects folder, HEAD file in .git/refs folder will have a file naming branch name master with latest commit hash key inside it.

What happens when there is a change in same file and its committed?

  • Again the same process repeats which will create new blob, commit and tree with their own hash keys.
  • Commit will hold information of previous commit key.

What happens when New Branch Created and checked out?

  • .git/refs/HEAD folder will have another file with branch name and latest commit hash key derived from the parent branch

  • HEAD in .git folder will point to that branch.

Wrapping Up

I hope this article gives you a clear understanding on how Git works on back scenes. And hopefully you will be aware of most crucial parts of the git after reading this article.

If you have reached this point, thanks for reading. I am Abilash S, You can check my latest posts regarding technology in these platforms. Feel Free to connect in any of these platforms

CodeHunt Channel Youtube

Abi Aradhya - Medium

JavaScript is not available.

item.title: Ng-新闻：第 22/39 集 | Ng-News: Episode 22/39
item.description:
在模板中调用函数是一种已知的代码异味。 我们不应该那样做。 该函数将不必要地运行多次，因为更改检测会触发它。

与往常一样，该规则也有例外。 其中之一是管道。 默认情况下，它是一个带有内置缓存的普通函数。

作为奖励，他解释了原始源代码，Angular 编译器从他的示例中生成。

在他的最新文章中，Tim Deschryver 指出了潜在的安全风险以及我们在使用 HTTP 拦截器时如何处理它们。 特别是当拦截器将身份验证令牌添加到 HTTP 标头时。

默认情况下，每个端点都会收到这个令牌，这肯定不是最好的方式。

另一篇有趣的文章来自 Dany Parades。 他讨论了复合组件模式。

每当组件在使用或可视化方面应该灵活时，您都可以将其拆分为多个部分，使用内容投影或@ContentChild 等技术协同工作。

正如他们所说，时间过得真快。 如果您喜欢历史或怀旧，您可以阅读 Daniel Rosenwasser 的周年纪念博文，或观看 Anders Hejlsberg 在 GOTO 会议上首次向世界展示 TypeScript。

除此之外，我们还有新的次要版本。 次要版本发布

发行说明

发行说明

Nx 14.8: Release Notes Nx 14.8：发行说明



原文:

Enea Jahollari: It's ok to use function calls in Angular templates

Calling functions within a template is a known code smell. We shouldn't do that. The function will unnecessarily run multiple times because the change detection triggers it.

As always, there are exceptions to the rule. One of them is the pipe. By default, it is a normal function with a built-in cache.

Enea Jahollari explains in his article how the pipe works and how we can apply the same principles to our own functions.

As a bonus, he explains the raw source code, the Angular compiler produces out of his example.

Tim Deschryver: Watch out what you expose with Angular Interceptors

In his latest article, Tim Deschryver points out potential security risks and how to deal with them when we use HTTP interceptors. especially when an interceptor adds authentication tokens to the HTTP headers.

By default, every endpoint receives this token which is surely not the best way.

https://timdeschryver.dev/blog/watch-out-what-you-expose-with-angular-interceptors

Dany Paredes: How to build Compound Components in Angular

Another interesting article comes from Dany Parades. He discusses the compound component pattern.

Whenever a component should be flexible in terms of its usage or visualisation, you can split it up into multiple pieces, all working together using techniques like content projection or @ContentChild, etc.

https://www.danywalls.com/how-to-build-compound-components-in-angular

TypeScript 10th year anniversary

As they say, time flies. TypeScript has already its 10-year anniversary. If you are in for history or nostalgia, you can read the anniversary blog post from Daniel Rosenwasser or watch Anders Hejlsberg at the GOTO conference where he presented TypeScript to the world for the first time.

Other than that, we have new minor versions. Jest, a testing framework, to 29.1 with improved support for types. Cypress, also a testing framework, to 10.9 with new features in the area of multi-domain switching. And nx, an alternative to the Angular CLI, to 14.8.

Minor Version Releases

Jest was released in 29.1 with improved support for type inference.

Release Notes

Cypress in 10.9 with new features for multi domain switching.

Release Notes

Nx 14.8: Release Notes
item.author: rainerhahnekamp

item.title: XPmarket - 9 月开发更新 | XPmarket - September Development Update
item.description:
尽管金融市场处于混乱状态，但我们的团队很高兴能够将全部注意力集中在我们的工作上并创造新的精彩事物。 此开发更新将引导您完成本月最激动人心的事情。

品牌重塑

当 xpmarket 推出时，它被构建为 MVP，以尽快测试这个想法。 因此，徽标也是由基本模板制成的。 现在，随着我们拥有数万人使用的产品和明确的方向 - 变革的时刻已经到来。 一支来自伦敦的才华横溢、充满激情的设计师团队创造了一个新的标志，我们希望它能够成为 XRPL 生态系统的标志。
您能看到我们的徽标与 xpmarket 和 XRPL 的关系吗？ 看一看，让你猜猜它传达了什么信息！

重新设计

门户的 MVP 与徽标类似，也是使用模板制作的，使我们能够快速高效地移动，在几个小时内创建新功能。 但是，这种方法在重负载下存在性能问题，UX 问题影响整体满意度，根本不符合我们的长期愿景。

我们与团队和专门从事应用程序环境设计的高级设计师 Regimantas 一起，检查了每个元素并重新设计了 React 上的代码。 现在 xpmarket 不再只是一个页面，而是一个性能显着提升，与多种设备兼容更好的应用程序。 打磨粗糙的边缘仍有大量工作，但我们技术精湛的开发团队确实很享受这个过程。 有什么比打造你真正喜欢的产品更重要的呢？

新域名

虽然 .io 域名在科技公司中广泛使用，但 A 级产品需要 A 级域名，特别是 .com 标准。 我们花时间收购了xpmarket.com ，该公司以前由英国的一家传统金融科技公司拥有。

本月，我们从技术方面发布了最先进的功能——集成到我们平台中的去中心化交易所（DEX）。 这是一个独特的解决方案，因为它在用户最需要它的关键领域与 xpmarket 进行了非常仔细和彻底的集成。

虽然 DEX 面向更复杂和高级的用户，或者那些想要更多控制权的用户，但当社区的其他成员只想进行快速交换时，我们会很感激。 认识我们的新功能 - 交换。 此外，它可以直接从任何令牌的页面访问！ 它也将很快作为独立页面发布。

市场追踪

现在，在中心化交易所上架的每个代币都会在代币页面上反映出来。

排名图

一个小但非常好的功能。 排名图表可在代币页面上找到，并显示代币在 xpmarket 上排名的历史数据。 此参数每天更新一次。

我们（三位创始人）都参加了 Apex 开发峰会——迄今为止，对于 XRPL 社区来说，这是最重要的活动。 这是那些建造、使用或希望潜入 XRPL 的人每年聚会的地方。 除了建立了不少联系之外，还有很多关于 XRPL 未来发展计划的消息。 我们学到了什么？

  • 与大多数事情一样 - AMM DEX 将在协议级别构建，这意味着最大的安全性、易用性和全球流动性。
  • 侧链——我们期待的东西。 这将允许几乎无限的可能性。 我们已经有一些计划，但稍后会公布！ 市场上有许多项目计划这样做，即铸造 NFT 并提供画廊。 我们之前有过这样的计划，但我们很高兴我们花时间重新考虑我们的解决方案，以便我们能够为我们的用户带来最大的价值，并提供不仅对 XRPL 生态系统而且对全世界独一无二的东西的区块链。 此外，似乎我们可能会通过 NFT 解决方案在 XRPL 市场上赚到最多的钱。

我们在他们的 Developer Discord Chat 中由 RippleX 主持了一场精彩的聊天。 我们讨论了整个 XPmarket 的想法是如何开始的，以及我们对未来的看法。

https://twitter.com/RippleXDev/status/1574117534768533517 https://twitter.com/RippleXDev/status/1574117534768533517



原文:

Even though the financial markets are in a state of chaos, our team is excited to be able to focus our full attention on our work and creating new and wonderful things. This development update will guide you through the most exciting things this month.

Rebranding

When xpmarket launched it was built as an MVP to test the idea as quickly as possible. Hence, the logo was also made from a basic template. Now as we have a product used by tens of thousands of people and a clear direction - the time for change has come. A highly talented and passionate team of designers from London created a new logo that we hope will become an icon of the XRPL ecosystem.
Can you see how our logo relates to xpmarket and XRPL? Have a look, make your best guess what message it carries!

Redesign

The MVP of the portal, similar to the logo, was also made using a template, allowing us to move quickly and efficiently, creating new functionalities in a few hours. However, this approach had performance issues under the heavy load, UX problems impacting overall satisfaction, and simply did not meet our long-term vision.

Together with the team and our senior designer Regimantas, who specialises in application environment design, we went through every element and redesigned the code on React. Now xpmarket is no longer just a page, but an application that has significantly improved performance and better compatibility with multiple devices. There is still a significant amount of work to polish rough edges, but our highly skilled development team does really enjoy the process. What can be more important than building a product you actually like?

New domain

Although the .io domains are widely used among tech companies, A-level products require A-level domains, specifically .com standard. We took our time and acquired xpmarket.com, previously owned by a legacy FinTech company from the UK.

xpmarket DEX

This month we released the most advanced feature from the technical aspect - Decentralised Exchange (DEX) integrated into our platform. This is a unique solution, as it was very carefully and thoroughly integrated with xpmarket right in the key areas where the users will need it the most.

https://xpmarket.com/trade-list

xpmarket Swaps

While the DEX is aimed at more sophisticated and advanced users, or those who want more control, we appreciate when other members of the community want to make just a quick swap. Meet our new feature - Swaps. Moreover, it is accessible directly from any token’s page! It also will be released as a stand alone page soon.

Markets tracking

xpmarket is the first XRPL data aggregator that implemented CEX markets tracking. Now, every token that is listed on a centralised exchange has this reflected on the token’s page.

Rank chart

A small, but very nice feature. Rank chart is available on a token’s page and shows historical data on tokens ranking on xpmarket. This parameter is updated once per day.

Apex XRPL Dev Summit

We (all three founders) have attended Apex Dev Summit - by far the most important event for XRPL community. This is the place where those building, using, or looking to dive into XRPL meet every year. Apart from establishing quite a few connections, there was a lot of news surrounding future development plans for XRPL. What we have learned?

  • AMM DEX - the feature which will bring the trading of XRPL assets into the next level and brings DeFi even closer. As with most things - AMM DEX will be protocol level built, meaning maximum security, ease of use, and global liquidity.
  • Side chains - something we are looking forward to. This will allow near infinite possibilities. We already have some plans for this, but to be disclosed later! :)
  • NFT - the wait is almost over. XLS20d is around the corner and ready to be deployed. There are many projects on the market that are planning to do the same, i.e. to mint NFTs and provide galleries. We had such a plan before, but we are very happy that we have taken the time to rethink our solutions so that we can bring the most value to our users and offer something unique not only to the XRPL ecosystem, but also to the whole world of blockchain. Also, it seems that we will probably make the most money in the XRPL market from the NFT solution.

AMA with RippleX

We had an amazing chat hosted by RippleX at their Developer Discord Chat. We discussed how the whole XPmarket idea started and where we see our future.

https://twitter.com/RippleXDev/status/1574117534768533517
item.author: enchstyle

item.title: 使用 git remote 并添加到我的 SSG | Working with git remote and adding to my SSG
item.description:
大家好，

本周，我与Alex合作，为我们的 SSG 添加了一项新功能。 这个新功能是能够使用 JSON 配置文件来传递选项，而不是在命令行中手动输入选项。 我在Alex 的 repo上工作。

代码写起来并不难。 我添加了新选项-c和--config ，它们将使用 JSON 配置文件来获取生成 HTML 文件时所需的所有选项。 例如，配置文件的内容可能如下所示：

 { "input": "./site", "output": "./build", "stylesheet": "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/water.css@2/out/water.css", "lang": "fr" }

我所做的代码过程是首先检查配置文件是否存在并且是 JSON 格式，然后从文件中读取并将内容放入对象中，最后使用对象中的属性来获取所有选项。 我遇到的最大问题是确保不重复代码，因此我必须先更改代码中的一些内容，然后才能添加更改。

我从这个过程中学到的最重要的事情是使用了 Git 的几个新特性。 我第一次使用 Draft Pull Request 并发现该功能非常有用。 很酷的是，您可以不断地向 PR 添加提交，直到您认为它已准备好进行审查。 我还学习了如何使用 git remote，它用于处理您没有克隆的不同 repo。 我使用此功能将合作伙伴的工作从他的分支转移到我的计算机上，以便我可以查看和测试他的代码。

幸运的是，说明很清楚，所以我对这些新的 Git 功能没有任何问题，但我仍然觉得我需要练习它们。 我将在我的整个编程生涯中继续使用这些功能。

原文:

Hi everyone,

This week I worked with Alex to add a new feature to our SSGs. This new feature was the ability to use a JSON config file to pass in options instead of typing in the options manually in the command line. I worked on Alex's repo.

The code wasn't too difficult to write. I added new options -c and --config which would use a JSON config file to get all the options needed when generating the HTML file. For example, the contents of the config file could look like this:

{
  "input": "./site",
  "output": "./build",
  "stylesheet": "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/water.css@2/out/water.css",
  "lang": "fr"
}

The code process I did was to first check if the config file exists and is JSON formatted, then read from the file and put the contents into an object, and finally use the properties from the object to get all the options. The biggest issue I had was making sure not to duplicate code, so I had to change a few things in the code before I could add my changes.

The biggest thing I learned from this process was using a couple of new features from Git. I used the Draft Pull Request for the first time and found how useful that feature is. It's cool how you can continually add commits to the PR until you believe it is ready for review. I also learned how to use a git remote, which is used to work with a different repo that you didn't clone. I used this feature to get my partner's work form his branch onto my computer so that I could review and test his code.

Luckily, the instructions were pretty clear, so I had no problems with these new Git features, but I still feel I need practice with them. I'll continue to use these features throughout my programming career.

item.title: CSS-Only Type Grinding：将标记 (sm|md|etc) 转换为有用的 CSS 值 | CSS-Only Type Grinding: Casting Tokens (sm|md|etc) into Useful CSS Values
item.description:
准备好迎接全新的 CSS 魔法了吗？

如果您正在编写一个可共享的组件或库，并希望为您的开发用户提供一个具有 CSS 选项的 API，例如：

--size: sm; 这实际上可以在 CSS 中完成，而无需涉及 JS、构建系统或样式/容器查询，这要归功于我命名为“类型研磨”的新技术。

编辑下面的标记化 CSS 值，看看感觉如何：

根据caniuse 的数据，当前全球用户支持率为 72.77%——特别是基于铬的浏览器。

它是如何工作的？！

我工作的公司，Bitovi，非常支持我，让我放弃我正在做的事情来写一篇关于这个的文章！ 所以在公司博客上，我将解构这里涉及的 CSS 来教授它是如何工作的，让人们意识到什么是可能的，并希望让你自己动手！

请看一下 - 我很想看看你用它做什么！

很高兴在下面、 Twitter或完整博客文章的评论中回答问题！

谢谢！ 一旦暂停，janeori 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，janeori 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，janeori 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 janeori 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 janeori 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 janeori 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Ready for brand new CSS magic?

If you're writing a sharable component or library and want to provide your dev users an API that has CSS options like:

--size: sm; | --size: md; | --size: lg;

it's actually possible to do in CSS, without involving JS, a build system, or Style/Container Queries thanks to a new technique I've named "Type Grinding."

Type Grinding allows your design tokens (keywords, or “<custom-ident>” values) written in your CSS to be transformed into any other values - like width, padding, color, etc - without relying on anything outside of CSS.

Edit the tokenized CSS values below to see how it feels:

The current global user support is 72.77% according to caniuse - specifically chromium based browsers.

How does it work?!

The company I work for, Bitovi, is super supportive and allowed me to drop what I was working on to write an article about this instead! So over on the company blog, I'll be destructuring the CSS involved here to teach how it works, bring awareness to what's possible, and hopefully empower you to do it yourself!

Please take a look - I would LOVE to see what you do with it!

Happy to answer questions below, on Twitter, or in the comments at the full blog post!

Thank you! 👽💜

item.title: 2022 年啤酒节 | Hacktoberfest 2022
item.description:
托马斯·考尔

发表于我们很高兴第一次与Ghostfolio一起加入Hacktoberfest ，并结识新的和雄心勃勃的开源贡献者。 每年 10 月，来自世界各地的开源维护者都会特别关注新的贡献者，同时指导他们完成他们在GitHub 上的第一个拉取请求。

关于 Ghostfolio

该软件实时呈现当前资产并支持未来投资的决策。 无论是重新平衡您投资组合的资产类别（股票、ETF、加密货币等）还是为公寓融资，Ghostfolio 都能提供可靠的、数据驱动的决策支持。

后端基于NestJS ，使用PostgreSQL作为数据库以及Prisma和Redis进行缓存。 前端是用Angular构建的。

如何贡献？

每一项贡献都很重要。 这可以是实现新功能、修复错误、重构代码、改进文档、添加更多单元测试或翻译成另一种语言。

您还不熟悉我们的代码库吗？ 那不是问题。 我们将hacktoberfest标签应用于一些非常适合新手的问题和 想法。

得到支持

如果您还有其他问题或想法，请加入我们不断壮大的Slack 社区或在 Twitter 上联系@ghostfolio_ 。

我们期待您的回音。
来自 Ghostfolio 的托马斯

取消标记 dtslvr 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Thomas Kaul

Posted on Oct 3

We are very excited to join Hacktoberfest for the first time with Ghostfolio and meet new and ambitious open-source contributors. Hacktoberfest is a month-long celebration of open-source projects, their maintainers, and the entire community of contributors. Each October, open source maintainers from all over the world give extra attention to new contributors while guiding them through their first pull requests on GitHub.

About Ghostfolio

Ghostfolio is a modern web application to manage your personal finance. The software presents the current assets in real time and supports the decision making of future investments. Whether rebalancing the asset classes (stocks, ETFs, cryptocurrencies, etc.) of your portfolio or financing an apartment, Ghostfolio offers solid, data-driven decision support.

Ghostfolio is written in TypeScript and organized as an Nx workspace. The backend is based on NestJS using PostgreSQL as a database together with Prisma and Redis for caching. The frontend is built with Angular.

How to contribute?

Every contribution matters. This can be implementing new features, fixing bugs, refactoring the code, improving the documentation, adding more unit tests, or translating into another language.

Are you not yet familiar with our code base? That is not a problem. We have applied the label hacktoberfest to a few issues and ideas that are well suited for newcomers.

The official Hacktoberfest website provides some valuable resources for beginners to start contributing in open source.

Get support

If you have further questions or ideas, please join our growing Slack community or get in touch on Twitter @ghostfolio_.

We look forward to hearing from you.
Thomas from Ghostfolio

Once suspended, dtslvr will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, dtslvr will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by dtslvr will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If dtslvr is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag dtslvr:

dtslvr consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging dtslvr will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: dtslvr

item.title: Archethic 钱包：用户与开发者的视角 | Archethic Wallet: User vs Developer Perspective
item.description:
无需注册或 KYC，用户只需控制他们的服务和访问钥匙串，受不同的安全访问方法保护，如 PIN 码、密码、YubiKey 等设备和生物识别。

主要特点

  • 去中心化钥匙串管理
  • 多账户管理
  • 可替代代币的创建
  • 创建 NFT
  • 支持交易（发送和接收 UCO 代币、可替代代币和 NFT）
  • 近期交易清单
  • 获得的代币列表

安全

  • 使用密码、PIN、Yubicloud OTP、Face ID、Touch ID、Uniris Biometrics (2023) 进行安全访问
  • 定制
    • 支持英语和法语
    • 支持多种货币（仅查看，不代表多种加密货币钱包）
    • 多主题（9个主题可用）
    • 用户界面定制

    其它功能

    • 本地通知
    • 进入交易所购买 ERC20 UCO
    • 使用二维码或手机分享功能分享地址
    • 地址簿
    • 访问最新的 Archethic 博客文章

    我们使用基于Dart语言的Flutter开发了钱包。

    它受欢迎的主要原因是它支持跨平台应用程序的创建。 以下是 Flutter 的一些功能*

    适用于 Android、iOS、Windows、Linux、macOS、Web、扩展的单一代码库：这种方法简化并减少了开发时间、成本，并且维护也很容易。 基于 Flutter 的用户界面几乎可以安装在任何平台上。 它有自己的渲染引擎，允许开发人员在移动到另一个平台时保持 UI 不变。 因此，应用程序用户可以在各种平台上享受出色的原生体验。

    开源并由 Google 制作： Flutter 是开发人员的热门选择，因为它拥有巨大的社区支持。 可以在 Flutter 中找到可靠且经过良好测试的插件，以减轻用户身份验证缺陷、恶意代码注入和数据泄露等安全风险。

    性能：作为一个跨平台框架，与竞争对手相比，Flutter 提供了无与伦比的性能。 与 React Native 不同的是，Flutter 直接从原生库中渲染小部件，而不是在渲染之前将库和组件下载到设备上。

    该应用程序在哪些平台上可用？

    如何安装应用程序并保护您的资金？

    您现在可以在 Google Play 商店下载我们的钱包（Android 移动版 beta 版）和 Archethic 官方网站下载 macOS 版（ https://www.archethic.net/aewallet.html ）。

    在入职过程中，一个屏幕会邀请您从一系列 24 个单词（种子短语）中创建一个新钱包或恢复您已经拥有的钱包。

    在新钱包的情况下，以英语或法语提出一系列 24 个单词（种子短语）。 这允许你为你的钱包生成你的私钥和公钥。 这些话是目前在丢失手机或卸载钱包的情况下收回资金的唯一方法。 因此，必须将此种子短语隐藏在您的设备以外的其他媒体上。

    保存种子短语后，您就可以充分享受您的钱包了。

    在钱包恢复的情况下，您只需以正确的顺序输入助记词的 24 个单词，应用程序就会从存储在区块链上的信息中找到您的钥匙串和相关帐户。

    开发者视角👨‍💻

    创建钱包时，会从 Dart 提供的加密安全随机数生成器中随机创建一个种子。

     static String generateSeed() { String result = ''; const String chars = 'abcdef0123456789'; final Random rng = Random.secure(); for (int i = 0; i < 64; i++) { result += chars[rng.nextInt(chars.length)]; } return result.toUpperCase(); }

    为了使种子更易于访问，使用了 BIP39，旨在提供一种使用一系列助记词来简化种子阅读的方法。 在 Archethic 钱包的情况下，我们使用 24 个词，因为词数越多，归于结果句子的熵和安全性就越大。 这些词是从包含 2048 个词的词典中选出的。 因此，flutter 库bip39_mnemonic与英语和法语词典一起使用。

    在钱包恢复的情况下，库bip39_mnemonic允许从助记词中找到种子。

    如何保护应用程序？

    对应用程序的访问以及与区块链的交互是安全的，因此某些操作不会超出用户的控制范围。

    以下是访问方式：

    • 通过PIN 码，由 6 位数字组成，可以混合键盘的数字，以减少通过观察访问您的代码的风险，
    • 通过密码，
    • 通过使用YubiKey ，它是一种电子身份验证设备，可保护您的访问。 无论您是在手机上使用 NFC 还是在台式机上使用 USB，您都可以通过此密钥识别自己，
    • 通过触摸 ID 或面部 ID 系统，取决于您设备的功能。

    开发者视角👩🏽‍💻

    在PIN和密码的情况下，身份验证信息存储在本地（请参阅“我的设备中存储了哪些数据？”）。
    在touch ID或Face ID的情况下，Flutter Local Auth library 提供了在支持指纹或面部识别身份验证的设备上进行身份验证的方法。

    最后，对于通过Yubicloud的OTP ：Yubico OTP 是一种简单而强大的身份验证机制，所有 YubiKey 都支持开箱即用。 在使用 YubiCloud 之前，您需要从这里获取一个 API 密钥 - 它快速、免费并且可以帮助我们防止滥用 YubiCloud。
    我为 Dart/Flutter 开发了一个Yubico dart 库，以便在我的个人 GitHub 存储库中与 Yubicloud 交互。

    我的设备中存储了哪些数据？

    我们存储一组信息，以便应用程序在在线和离线模式下都可以正常工作。

    第一组信息代表全局可用的用户偏好：

    • 首次启动：允许知道这是否是应用程序的首次启动，以便清除 iOS 的密钥库。 事实上，iOS 密钥库是持久的，所以如果这是第一次启动，那么我们将清除密钥库。
    • 身份验证方法：允许知道使用哪种身份验证模式（PIN、密码、YubiKey、生物识别）。
    • 当前货币：让您知道应用程序中使用的货币。
    • 当前语言：允许您了解应用程序中使用的语言。
    • 当前主要设置：允许知道优先显示的货币是法定货币还是加密货币。
    • 当前网络：允许了解钱包是否连接到主网、测试网或连接到其端点记录在以下信息中的本地节点。
    • 当前网络端点：允许知道本地节点的地址。
    • 当前主题：允许知道应用程序中使用了哪个主题。
    • 锁定超时：例如，如果用户让应用程序打开以浏览另一个应用程序，则允许知道应用程序在启动时需要多长时间进行身份验证。
    • 显示余额：允许您确定是否应显示财务信息。
    • 显示博客：让您知道应该显示哪些博客文章。
    • 显示价格图表：允许您确定是否应显示 UCO 价格图表的图形和指标。
    • 主动振动：允许知道在每个动作中，手机是否会发出小振动。
    • 活动通知：允许知道接收 UCO 的通知是否处于活动状态。
    • 语言种子：允许知道种子短语是由法语还是英语单词组成。

    第二组信息代表与安全相关的敏感元素：

    • 种子：允许存储在 Archethic 区块链上执行交易所需的钱包种子。
    • 密码：允许保留密码以防选择此身份验证方法。
    • 此信息被安全存储。

      最后，一个安全的 256 位（32 字节）加密密钥用于保护磁盘上的数据。
      隐私政策可在我们的网站上找到。

      开发者视角👨🏻‍💻

      对于用户偏好数据，出于性能原因，我们选择将其存储在本地数据库Hive中，而不是 Flutter 对象SharedPreferences中。 所有信息都可以在这篇媒体文章中找到。

      这些数据以明文形式存储，因为它们不敏感。

      另一方面，必须保护与安全管理相关的信息。 这里再次使用 Hive，但存储的值是加密的。 密钥通过FlutterSecureStorage库以 64 位编码存储在安全空间中。

       static Future<Vault> getInstance() async { try { const FlutterSecureStorage secureStorage = FlutterSecureStorage(); final Uint8List encryptionKey; String? secureKey = await secureStorage.read(key: 'archethic_wallet_secure_key'); if (secureKey == null || secureKey.isEmpty) { final List<int> key = Hive.generateSecureKey(); encryptionKey = Uint8List.fromList(key); secureKey = base64UrlEncode(key); await secureStorage.write( key: 'archethic_wallet_secure_key', value: secureKey); } else { encryptionKey = base64Url.decode(secureKey); } final Box<dynamic> encryptedBox = await Hive.openBox<dynamic>(_vaultBox, encryptionCipher: HiveAesCipher(encryptionKey)); return Vault._(encryptedBox); } catch (e) { throw Exception(); } }

      钱包如何与 Archethic 区块链交互？

      开发者视角👩‍💻

      基于 Archethic JS SDK，在 dart 中开发了一个 SDK ，并由 Archethic 团队维护，以提供内部或社区开发的 Flutter DApp 与 Archethic 区块链交互。

      这个开源 SDK “ archethic_lib_dart ”，在GitHub上可用，可以通过 Flutter 库和包共享站点Pub.dev作为依赖项添加到 Dart 或 Flutter 项目中。

      可用的功能有：

      • 密码功能，
      • 交易大楼，
      • 远程端点调用，
      • 钥匙串/钱包管理，
      • 所有文档都可以在库的自述文件中找到。

        钱包时间线

        作者观点👷

        到目前为止，Archethic 社区一直参与钱包的开发，以改善技术方面和用户体验。 钱包的使命是所有人都可以访问以简化和保护使用。

        您可以通过GitHub 上的专用存储库或通过应用程序的官方页面关注或为应用程序的开发做出贡献。

        作者：Sylvain Séramy，Archethic Technologies 前端部门负责人



原文:

Archethic has developed a fully decentralized and non-custodial cryptocurrency hot wallet that enables users to safely manage assets on Layer 1 Archethic blockchain.

No signup or KYC needed, users just control their services and access keychain, protected by different secure access methods like PIN Code, Password, YubiKey like devices and Biometrics.

What are the features of the Archethic wallet?

Archethic Wallet has implemented the following features:

Main features

  • Decentralized keychain management
  • Multiple accounts' management
  • Creation of Fungible Tokens
  • Creation of NFTs
  • Support for transactions (Sending and Receiving UCO Token, Fungible Tokens and NFTs)
  • List of recent transactions
  • List of acquired tokens

Security

  • Security access with Password, PIN, Yubicloud OTP, Face ID, Touch ID, Uniris Biometrics (2023)
  • Use of 24 Words Mnemonics

Customization

  • Support for English and French Language
  • Support for multiple Currencies (view only, not meant as multiple cryptocurrencies wallet)
  • Multi themes (9 themes available)
  • UI customization

Other features

  • Local notifications
  • Access to exchanges to buy ERC20 UCO
  • Share address with QR Code or mobile share feature
  • Address book
  • UCO Price chart
  • Access latest Archethic blog articles

How is the Archethic wallet developed?

We developed the wallet using Flutter based on Dart language.

Flutter is an open-source mobile application development framework from Google. The main reason for its popularity is that it supports the creation of cross-platform applications. Flutter is also used to create interactive apps that run on web pages or on the desktop.

Here are some of Flutter's features*

Single code base for Android, iOS, Windows, Linux, macOS, Web, Extension: This approach simplifies and reduces the development time, cost, and maintenance is also an easy task. The Flutter-based user interface can be installed virtually on any platform. It has its own rendering engine that allows developers to keep the UI as it is while moving to another platform. As a result, application users can enjoy an excellent native-like experience on various platforms.

Open-source and made by Google: Flutter is a popular choice among developers because of the huge community support. Google designed the Flutter framework with all the security issues of modern applications in mind. One can find reliable and well-tested plugins in Flutter to mitigate security risks such as user authentication flaws, malicious code injections and data leaks.

Dart Programming: Flutter uses an easy to learn and implement programming language called Dart, which is Google's general purpose programming language.

Performance: As a cross-platform framework, Flutter offers unmatched performance compared to its competitors. Flutter compiles designs to native code. Unlike React Native, Flutter renders widgets directly from the native library rather than downloading libraries and components to the device before rendering.

*Source: https://mobiskill.fr/blog/conseils-emploi-tech/pourquoi-utiliser-flutter-en-2022/

On which platforms is the application available?

How to install the application and secure your funds?

You can now download our wallet on the Google Play Store in beta for the Android mobile version and via the official website of Archethic for the macOs version (https://www.archethic.net/aewallet.html).

During the onboarding process, a screen invites you to create a new wallet or to restore a wallet you already own, from a series of 24 words (seed phrase).

In the case of a new wallet, a series of 24 words (seed phrase) is proposed in English or French. This allows you to generate your private and public keys for your wallet. These words are currently the only way to recover your funds in case of loss of your mobile or uninstallation of the wallet. It is therefore essential to keep this seed phrase hidden from view and on some medium other than your devices.

⚠️ Remember that Archethic will never ask you for your 24 words series.

Once the seed phrase is saved, you can fully enjoy your wallet.

In the case of a wallet restoration, you just have to enter the 24 words of your seed phrase in the right order and the application will find your keychain and associated accounts, from the information stored on the blockchain.

The developer's perspective 👨‍💻

When the wallet is created, a seed is randomly created from a cryptographically secure random number generator provided by Dart.

static String generateSeed() {
    String result = '';
    const String chars = 'abcdef0123456789';
    final Random rng = Random.secure();
    for (int i = 0; i < 64; i++) {
      result += chars[rng.nextInt(chars.length)];
    }
    return result.toUpperCase();
  }

In order to make the seed more accessible, BIP39 is used and aims to provide a method of simplifying the reading of the seed using a series of mnemonic words. BIP39 takes random words (usually from the English language, although they can be from another language) and creates a long phrase with them, usually 12 to 24 words. In the case of the Archethic wallet, we use 24 words because the higher the number of words, the greater the entropy and security attributable to the resulting sentence. These words are chosen from a dictionary of 2048 words. The flutter library bip39_mnemonic is thus used with English and French dictionaries.

In the case of wallet restoration, the library bip39_mnemonic allows to find the seed from the seed phrase.

How to secure the application?

Access to the application and interactions with the blockchain are secured so that certain actions are not done beyond the user's control.

Following are the ways to access:

  • by PIN code, comprising of 6 digits, with the possibility to mix the numbers of the keyboard to reduce the risks of access to your code by observation,
  • by password,
  • by using a YubiKey, it is an electronic authentication device to secure your access. Whether you are using NFC with your mobile or USB on your desktop, you can identify yourself with this key,
  • by the touch ID or face ID system, depends on the capabilities of your device.

The developer's perspective 👩🏽‍💻

In the case of PIN and password, authentication information is stored locally (see "What data is stored in my device?").
In the case of touch ID or Face ID, the Flutter Local Auth library provides the means to authenticate on devices supporting fingerprint or facial recognition authentication.

Finally, for OTP via Yubicloud: Yubico OTP is a simple yet strong authentication mechanism that is supported by all YubiKeys out of the box. Yubico OTP can be used as the second factor in a 2-factor authentication scheme or on its own, providing 1-factor authentication. YubiCloud is the name of Yubico’s web service for verifying OTPs. Before using YubiCloud, you need to get an API key from here - it is quick, free and helps us in preventing misuse of YubiCloud.
I developed a Yubico dart library for Dart/Flutter to interact with Yubicloud in my personal GitHub repository.

What data is stored in my device?

We store a set of information so that the application is functional in both online and offline mode.

The first group of information represents the globally available user preferences:

  • First Launch: Allows to know if this is the first launch of the application in order to clear the keystore for iOS. Indeed, iOS key store is persistent, so if this is first launch then we will clear the keystore.
  • Authentification Method: Allows to know which authentication mode is used (PIN, password, YubiKey, biometrics).
  • Current Currency: Allows you to know what currency is used in the application.
  • Current Language: Allows you to know which language is used in the application.
  • Current Primary Setting: Allows to know if the currency displayed in priority is Fiat or Crypto.
  • Current Network: Allows to know if the wallet is connected to mainnet, testnet or to a local node whose endpoint is recorded in the following information.
  • Current Network Endpoint: Allows to know the address of the local node.
  • Current Theme: Allows to know which theme is used in the application.
  • Lock: Allows to know if it is necessary to authenticate at the launching of the application.
  • Lock Timeout: Allows to know after how long the application requires authentication at its launch if the user has left the application open to browse for another one for example.
  • Pin Pad Shuffle: Allows you to determine whether the PIN code keyboard should be shuffled when entering.
  • Show Balances: Allows you to determine if the financial information should be displayed.
  • Show Blog: Allows you to know which blog articles should be displayed.
  • Show Price Chart: Allows you to determine whether the graph and indicators of the UCO price chart should be displayed.
  • Active Vibrations: Allows to know if at each action, a small vibration is emitted on the mobiles.
  • Active Notifications: Allows to know if the notifications of reception of UCO are active or not.
  • Language Seed: Allows to know if the seed phrase is composed of French or English words.

The second group of information represents the sensitive elements related to security:

  • Seed: Allows to store the wallet seed needed to perform transactions on the Archethic blockchain.
  • PIN: Allows to store the PIN code in case this authentication method has been chosen.
  • Password: Allows to keep the password in case this authentication method has been chosen.
  • Yubicloud ID and API Key: Allows to keep the authentication information for the management of the OTP with the YubiKey in case this authentication method has been chosen.

This information is stored securely.

And finally, a secure 256-bit (32 bytes) encryption key to secure data on the disk.
⚠️ Remember that Archethic will never pass on your data to a third party or use your data in any other way than that offered by the application.

The privacy policy is available on our website.

The developer's perspective 👨🏻‍💻

In the case of user preference data, we made the choice to store it in a local database Hive rather than the Flutter object SharedPreferences for performance reasons. All the information is available in this medium article.

These data are stored in clear text because they are not sensitive.

On the other hand, the information related to security management must be protected. Here again, Hive is used but the stored values are encrypted. Hive provides a helper function to generate a secure encryption key using the Fortuna random number generator.
The key is stored base 64 encoded in a secure space via the FlutterSecureStorage library.

static Future<Vault> getInstance() async {
    try {
      const FlutterSecureStorage secureStorage = FlutterSecureStorage();
      final Uint8List encryptionKey;
      String? secureKey =
          await secureStorage.read(key: 'archethic_wallet_secure_key');
      if (secureKey == null || secureKey.isEmpty) {
        final List<int> key = Hive.generateSecureKey();
        encryptionKey = Uint8List.fromList(key);
        secureKey = base64UrlEncode(key);
        await secureStorage.write(
            key: 'archethic_wallet_secure_key', value: secureKey);
      } else {
        encryptionKey = base64Url.decode(secureKey);
      }
      final Box<dynamic> encryptedBox = await Hive.openBox<dynamic>(_vaultBox,
          encryptionCipher: HiveAesCipher(encryptionKey));
      return Vault._(encryptedBox);
    } catch (e) {
      throw Exception();
    }
  }

How does the wallet interact with the Archethic blockchain?

The developer's perspective 👩‍💻

Based on the Archethic JS SDK, a SDK has been developed in dart and is maintained by the Archethic teams in order to offer internal or community-developed Flutter DApps to interact with the Archethic Blockchain.

This open-source SDK "archethic_lib_dart", available on GitHub, can be added as dependencies in Dart or Flutter projects via the Flutter libraries and packages sharing site Pub.dev.

The available functions are:

  • Cryptographic functions,
  • Transaction building,
  • Remote endpoint calls,
  • Keychain / Wallet management,
  • CoinGecko functions.

All the documentation is available in the readme of the library.

Wallet timeline

Some elements of the SDK and wallet history are shown here:

The author's perspective 👷

Until now, the Archethic community has always been involved in the development of the wallet to improve both the technical aspects and the user experience. The wallet has the vocation to be accessible to all to simplify and secure the uses.

You can follow or contribute to the development of the application via the dedicated repository on GitHub or via the official page of the application.

Author: Sylvain Séramy, Head of Front-End Department at Archethic Technologies
item.author: margarita_manzanera

item.title: 🛍️ Partenaires Mobilis : API, appli responsive 💻📱 & Open Data 🙀 | 🛍️ Partenaires Mobilis : API, appli responsive 💻📱 & Open Data 🙀
item.description:
用于opt-nc 的adriens

发表于拉斐尔·吉梅内斯·莱特 - 罗杰·维纳斯·爱尔康 - 一旦暂停，optnc 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

举报其他不当行为

原文:

adriens for opt-nc

Posted on Oct 3

L'OPT-NC met

"Un réseau de boutiques Mobilis à ma disposition"

☝️ Toutefois, le contenu proposé est non interopérable : il n'y a pas d'API ... pourtant ces données ont un potentiel non néligeable, comme par exemple des fonctionnalités liées à la Géolocalisation, voire l'intégration à des services tiers...

💡 L'idée

Nous avons donc entrepris de transformer ces données en une API interopérable, dynamique et enrichir avec :

  • 🔗 Les liens vers les pages de réseaux sociaux des partenaires (Facebook, Google Maps,...)
  • 🗺️ La possibilité de faire des recherches géolocalisées
  • 📱💻 Un front-end responsive qui met en valeur les possibilités de l'API
  • 🔌 Des clés interopérables afin que l'on puisse faire le lien avec des SI tiers (par exemple en Open Data communes-nc)
  • 🎁 et autres "goodies" à découvrir ☺️

🍿 Demo

Assez de blabla stratégique : feu sur la démo qui résume tout cela en 15' 👇

🔖 Ressources

Rafael Gimenes Leite - Sep 12

Roger Viñas Alcon - Sep 21

item.title: 将 SSH 密钥放入 Hashicorp Vault | Put an SSH Key into Hashicorp Vault
item.description:
弗雷德里克·奥林格

发表于本文假设您已经设置了最小保管库： https ://dev.to/frederickollinger/production-hashicorp-vault-minimal-configuration-485a

什么是秘密引擎

秘密引擎是保存秘密的地方。 由于各种原因，有许多秘密后端。 例如，有一个 SSL 证书和一个 SSH 证书的后端。

启用 SSH 密钥引擎

这是管理员在设置新的 Vault 实例时必须执行的一次性操作：

将现有的 SSH 密钥放入 Vault

假设我们有一个名为 fileserver.pem 的 ssh 密钥，将其放入保管库，如下所示：

 vault write ssh/keys/fileserver key = @fileserver.pem

鳍

这就是将密钥放入 Vault 所需的全部内容。

如果我们只有一个好的向导，有时事情比我们想象的要容易。

原文:

Frederick Ollinger

Posted on Oct 3

This article assumes that you have set up a minimum vault: https://dev.to/frederickollinger/production-hashicorp-vault-minimal-configuration-485a

What is a Secrets Engine

A secrets engine is a place to keep secrets. There are a number secrets backend for various reasons. For example, there is a backend for SSL certificates and one of SSH certificates.

Enable the SSH Secrets Engine

This is a one time operation that the admin must do when setting up a new Vault instance:

Put Existing SSH Key into Vault

Assuming we have an ssh key named fileserver.pem, put it into the vault like so:

vault write ssh/keys/fileserver key=@fileserver.pem

Fin

That's all that's needed to get a key into Vault.

Sometimes things are easier than we imagine if we only have a good guide.

item.title: Ruby：打电话给我（其中一只老狗学会了新把戏） | Ruby: Call me (in which an old dog learns new tricks)
item.description:
阿德里安·李

发表于几年前，我在某个地方工作，主要开发人员希望我们遵守 SRP 并通过名为call的单个接口公开该类，例如：

 def Foo def self.call = new.call def call = "hello" end

这实际上最终在某些方面创建了一个非常干净的代码库（当然有助于缓解命名问题），尽管忽略了更明显的约定，例如返回哈希时的to_h 、字符串的 to_s 等最终让我觉得它应用得太严格了。

不过，其中一个错失的机会是让人们更广泛地理解为什么这样的课程很有用，我最近有机会重新审视这个问题。

如果您不知道，有几个 ruby​​ 对象已经响应call ，即lambda和proc 。

 puts lambda { |x| x * x }.call(2) puts proc { |x| x + x }.(3)

您还可以使用method或public_method将方法转换为 proc：

 def foo(x) = x - x puts method(:foo).call(10)

考虑到这一点，您可以传递 lambda、proc、方法、类或类的实例，所有这些都使用相同的接口执行。

这是为期一个月的开源庆祝活动。 对于许多开发人员来说，这是他们对开源的介绍。

查看 DEV 上的 Hacktoberfest 标签以了解最新消息！

一旦暂停，os6sense 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，os6sense 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，os6sense 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 os6sense 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 os6sense 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Adrian Lee

Posted on Oct 3

A few years ago I worked somewhere where the lead dev wanted us to adhere to SRP and expose the class via a single interface named call e.g:

def Foo
  def self.call = new.call
  def call = "hello"
end

This actually ended up making a pretty clean codebase in certain respects (certainly helped ease the problem of naming things) although ignoring more obvious conventions such as to_h when returning a hash, to_s for strings etc eventually left me feeling it was too rigorously applied.

One of this missed opportunities though was in generating a broader understanding of why a class like this is useful and I recently had an opportunity to revisit the question.

In case you aren't aware, there are couple of ruby objects which already respond to call namely lambda and proc.

puts lambda { |x| x * x }.call(2)
puts proc { |x| x + x }.(3)

You can also covert a method into a proc using method or public_method:

def foo(x) = x - x
puts method(:foo).call(10)

With this in mind you can pass around a lambda, a proc, a method, a class, or an instance of a class, all executed with the same interface.

It is a month-long celebration of open source. For a lot of devs, its their introduction to open source.

Check out the Hacktoberfest tag on DEV to keep up with the latest!

Suraj Vishwakarma - Oct 3

item.title: Ordenação por flutuação - 冒泡排序 | Ordenação por flutuação - Bubble Sort
item.description:
丹尼尔·多明

发表于例：

 //Um array desordenado var arr = [ 2 , 3 , 6 , 1 , 5 , 7 ] //Um array ordenado var arr = [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 5 , 6 , 7 ]

示例：

 var arr = [ 2 , 3 , 6 , 1 , 5 , 7 ]

  • 一旦暂停，dormin 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 dormin 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Daniel Dormin

Posted on Oct 3

Começando meus estudos de algoritmos cai no Bubble sort.
Vamos entender o que é um algoritmo para começar nossa caminhada de estudo.

Em matemática e ciência da computação, um algoritmo é uma sequência finita de ações executáveis que visam obter uma solução para um determinado tipo de problema. Segundo Dasgupta, Papadimitriou e Vazirani; "Algoritmos são procedimentos precisos, não ambíguos, padronizados, eficientes e corretos." - Wikipédia

Esse trecho do wikipédia é o suficiente para entendermos o que é um algoritmo. Sabendo disso podemos passar para o algoritmo de estudo, o bubble sort, como o nome diz esse é um algoritmo de ordenação, ou seja o objetivo dele é ordenar uma estrutura de dados como um array. Por exemplo:

//Um array desordenado
var arr = [ 2, 3, 6, 1, 5, 7]
//Um array ordenado
var arr = [1, 2 , 3, 5, 6, 7]

Neste exemplo o array está ordenado de forma crescente mas a ordenação pode ser feita conforme a necessidade.
O bubble sort reorganiza o array para que a ordenação seja feita.

Como o Bubble sort faz isso?

O bubble sort funciona comparando de dois em dois valores, trocando-os de lugar do maior pro menor ou do menor pro maior por meio de um loop e uma condicional. exemplo:

var arr = [ 2, 3, 6, 1, 5, 7]

  • Na primeira comparação ele compara o primeiro e o segundo valor (2 e 3), não há nada para trocar ele passa para a próxima dupla.

  • Na segunda volta ele compara o segundo e o terceiro valor (3 e 6), também não precisa ser alterado.

  • Na terceira volta compara o terceiro e o quarto valor (6, 1), aqui os valores são trocados assim os organizando (1, 6).

E assim continua até que o maior valor fique totalmente à direita, depois ele volta fazendo de novo a comparação, até o segundo maior valor está organizado, seguindo até que todo o array esteja organizado. Neste ponto podemos perceber que precisamos de dois loops, um loop para mais interno para comparar todos os valores e reorganizar o array e um mais externo para que ele faça isso para cada item do array. O código fica assim:

let lista = [17, 2, 3, 4, 5]
let numero = 0
console.log(lista)
for (let i = 0; i <= lista.length - 1; i++) { for (let i = 0; i <= lista.length - 1; i++) { if (lista[i] >= lista[i + 1]) { numero = lista[i] lista[i] = lista[i + 1] lista[i + 1] = numero } } }
console.log(lista)

Fiz uma demonstração, caso queira conferir o link é: https://codepen.io/ddparkas/pen/JjpVJdb

Muito obrigado por ler até aqui abaixo deixo as referências usadas:
https://pt.wikipedia.org/wiki/Algoritmo
https://pt.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bubble_sort

item.title: #100daysofcode - 第 2 天 | #100daysofcode - Day 2
item.description:
穆罕默德·J·侯赛因

发表于了解第 1 天

是的！ 我熬过了第二天！！

成就

今天我开始更深入地研究 Express。 开始阅读路由器并使用 Express Router 创建了 2 个 api 路由。 如果我必须向某人解释 Express Router，我会简单地说：

它帮助您摆脱复杂的 api 路由，并帮助您更有效地划分和管理每条路由。

进步

就我的“梦想项目”而言，我使用 Express Router 创建了两个主要的 api 端点。 以前，我没有使用路由器，事情有点复杂，甚至代码看起来有点混乱。 感谢快递路由器，我终于可以满足我的强迫症A**了。

挑战

不过，没有任何重大挑战，我花了一点时间来理解 Express Router 的概念并将其应用到我当前的项目中。

Mohammed J Hossain

Posted on Oct 3

Read about Day 1

Yes! I made it through Day 2!!

Accomplishments

Today I started diving deeper into Express. Started reading about Router and created 2 api routes using Express Router. If I had to explain Express Router to someone I'd simply say:

It helps you from complicated api routes and helps you divide and manage each route more efficiently.

Progress

In terms of my "Dream Project" I have created two of my main api endpoints using Express Router. Previously, I hadn't used router and things were a bit more complicated and even the code looked a lil bit messed up. Thanks to express router, I can finally satisfy my OCD A**.

Challenges

There weren't any significant challenges, though, it took a little bit of time to understand the concept of Express Router and apply it to my current project.

原文:

This communiqué originally appeared on Symfony Station, your source for cutting-edge Symfony and PHP news.

Welcome to this week's Symfony Station Communiqué. It's your review of the essential news in the Symfony and PHP development communities. We also cover the cybersecurity world in detail.

This edition was produced under duress due to Hurricane Ian, so it’s late.

Take your time and enjoy the items most relevant and valuable to you.

As always, thanks to Javier Eguiluz and Symfony for sharing our last communiqué in their Week of Symfony.

My opinions will be in bold.

Many of the items we curate are on Medium. I recommend investing in membership as you can access everything you want to read. It’s a small investment in boosting your career. As you may have noticed, non-members can only access a limited number of articles per month.

Become a member here! The compensation we receive from your use of this link helps pay for our weekly communiqué.

Symfony

As always, we will start with the official news from Symfony.

Highlight -> “This week, Symfony development activity focused on finishing and polishing some new features for the upcoming Symfony 6.2 version, such as: updating codebase to use modern PHP features like null coalescing assignment and match statements; adding new config options
for HttpCache; and updating some new panels in the Symfony Profiler.“

A Week of Symfony #821 (19-25 September 2022)

Symfony announced:

Twig security release: Possibility to load a template outside a configured directory when using the filesystem loader

SymfonyCon Disneyland Paris 2022: From monolith to decoupled…wait, why is that one getting bigger?!

SymfonyCon Disneyland Paris 2022: Calculating what we can’t see: carbon emissions in the cloud

SymfonyCon Disneyland Paris 2022: Voice of partner panel discussion: how Platform.sh agency partners drive innovation and growth

Also, be sure to join our newsletter list at the bottom of our site’s pages. Joining gets you each week's communiqué in your inbox (a day early).

If you don't already, follow us on Twitter at @symfonfystation.

And since it may be turning into a full-scale dumpster fire, we are now on Mastodon as well at @symfonystation@phpc.social. Consider joining the @phpc.social instance.
